what_it_does: A findings document reporting the result of a by-use experiment: using small models to scan the whole repo for vocabulary that is hardcoded but should be registry-driven. It records the multi-tier composition that worked (deterministic extract -> deterministic overlap set-compare -> a single 4B model kind-call -> escalate-to-Tim candidacy), what the test proved about model unreliability, the concrete TIER-1 deterministic violations found, UNIFICATION duplicate findings, the TIER-3 items escalated to Tim, and the backlog this produced.
observations: Frames the experiment as Tim's test (2026-06-09) with his warning that small models can't make judgement/subjective calls freely

Documents a four-tier composition: TIER-1 deterministic extract (39 candidate literals), TIER-2a deterministic set-compare overlap (the fix), TIER-2b a single 4B kind-call (vocabulary vs incidental), TIER-3 escalate-to-Tim candidacy
Reports the loose model-overlap run hallucinated 7 overlaps; the tightened deterministic set-compare gave 3 findings with zero hallucination

Reports the 4B kind-call labelled 34/39 literals 'vocabulary' including field-name tuples, method pairs, CLI verbs, booleans — concluding the model tier adds noise and the deterministic tier carries the signal
TIER-1 findings cite specific file:line violations: runtime/suite.py:2231 and :5653 (sampling-keys subset), runtime/authoring.py:494 (subset of SCHEMES)
UNIFICATION findings list built-twice/thrice vocabulary: sampling-keys x3, ('plan','apply') x2, ('propose','panel','extend') x2, framed against LAW-0

TIER-3 escalation lists genuine domain-vocabulary candidates (origin polarity, status lifecycles, run-states, reduce-modes, op-axis) and dismisses field-name tuples / CLI verbs / booleans as incidental
Ends with a backlog: fix the 3 deterministic violations + 3 dups, and a SYSTEM-GAPS composition lesson
standouts: The earned lesson is explicit and counterintuitive: in registry-coverage the model's contribution is marginal and the deterministic set-compare carries the signal — recommends possibly dropping the model kind-call for deterministic shape-heuristics

Two independent confirmations of the thesis that small models can't make these calls: deterministic overlap beat model overlap (zero vs 7 hallucinations), and the narrow binary kind-call was still unreliable (34/39 mislabeled)
Concrete cited file:line evidence for each TIER-1 violation rather than abstract claims
conventions: Uses the repo's registry-is-truth / no-hardcoding framing and LAW-0 unification language
Separates deterministic-confidence findings from model-noise and from operator-escalation explicitly (a tiered honesty convention)
Marks the operator-only candidacy call as Tim's, not the agent's
notes: This is the evidence file proving that, for finding hardcoded-but-should-be-registry vocabulary, the deterministic set-compare is the reliable signal and the small-model judgement call is near-worthless — plus a concrete list of cited violations to fix.
questions: Have the 3 cited TIER-1 violations and 3 duplicates been fixed since this was written, or are they still open in the backlog?
Did Tim make the TIER-3 candidacy calls on the escalated domain-vocabulary candidates?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it states it is the by-use result plus the composition lesson earned, and the body delivers exactly that (the working composition, the proofs, the cited findings, the escalations, the backlog).
intention_signals: Trying to empirically settle where model judgement helps versus harms in a registry-coverage scan, and to convert that into a reusable composition rule (push judgement to deterministic set-compare, keep candidacy with the operator) plus an actionable list of cited violations.
novelty: The dual-proof framing that even a narrowed binary model call is unreliable, and the resulting design recommendation to demote/drop the model tier in favor of deterministic shape-heuristics for vocabulary detection.
contribution: Contributes the empirical composition lesson for registry-coverage scanning (deterministic-overlap-primary, model-kind-call-marginal, operator-candidacy) plus a cited inventory of specific hardcoded-vocabulary violations and duplicates to remediate.
summary_for_embedding: A findings document from a whole-repo scan for hardcoded-but-should-be-registry vocabulary, reporting that deterministic set-compare overlap carries the signal (3 findings, zero hallucination) while the small-model kind-call is noise (34/39 mislabeled), with cited TIER-1 violations and unification duplicates.
intention_for_embedding: Written to empirically determine where small-model judgement helps versus harms in registry-coverage detection and to record the reusable composition lesson plus the concrete cited violations to fix.
